content.claim: In an Ipsos survey (N=280 households with children), 75% reported that renewed school/kindergarten closure would create serious difficulty, and 78% of most-affected households are in small rural towns or villages.
content.context: Closing small-village primary schools in depopulating Hungarian settlements is not justified as an efficiency measure on current evidence, because per-pupil cost premiums are economically defensible, learning outcomes do not suffer materially, and closure-induced local population loss and community harm systematically outweigh accounting savings.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
